implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: memory_supersede MCP tool implementation with validation
Unit tests covering all validation cases and error handling
Integration tests verifying search exclusion and forward supersession

completion_promises.criterion_text: src/fleet_memory/mcp/tools/supersede.py exists and registers memory_supersede via the TASK-MCP-001 extension point
completion_promises.evidence: File exists with register() function that registers memory_supersede_tool with FastMCP server. Verified by reading the file - it implements the extension point pattern used by other MCP tools.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Declaring that a newer memory supersedes an older one marks the older as superseded_by the newer; the older no longer appears in default search results
completion_promises.evidence: Integration test test_superseded_memory_drops_out_of_default_search verifies that: (1) predecessor is marked with superseded_by field, (2) predecessor is excluded from default search (include_superseded=False), (3) predecessor appears when include_superseded=True. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A single-predecessor declaration is accepted and marks that one predecessor
completion_promises.evidence: Unit test test_single_predecessor_accepted verifies single predecessor is processed correctly. Integration test also uses single predecessor. Tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: An empty predecessor list is rejected with 'at least one predecessor is required' (ASSUM-005) — not a silent no-op
completion_promises.evidence: Unit test test_empty_predecessor_list_rejected verifies that empty list triggers ValueError with message 'At least one predecessor is required'. Implementation validates at line 65-66. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A malformed predecessor reference (e.g. not-a-key) is rejected with 'not a valid memory key' and no supersession is applied
completion_promises.evidence: Unit test test_malformed_predecessor_rejected verifies malformed keys are rejected with 'not a valid memory key' message. Helper function _validate_natural_key checks format. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A memory superseding itself is rejected with 'a memory cannot supersede itself' and no supersession is applied
completion_promises.evidence: Unit tests test_self_supersession_rejected and test_self_supersession_in_list_rejected verify self-supersession detection. Implementation checks at line 84-85. Tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Forward supersession: declaring supersession of a predecessor that has not been written is accepted and takes effect once it is written
completion_promises.evidence: Unit test test_forward_supersession_accepted and integration test test_forward_supersession_takes_effect_after_write verify forward supersession is accepted. The underlying apply_supersessions function handles non-existent predecessors gracefully. Tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the store raises TimeoutError the tool returns 'memory store unavailable' and the server stays running (via TASK-MCP-002)
completion_promises.evidence: Unit test test_store_timeout_degrades_gracefully verifies TimeoutError is caught by @tool_safe decorator and converted to infrastructure error with 'memory store' message. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'ruff check tests/integration/test_mcp_supersede.py' - result: 'All checks passed!'
